name: Proto-Mashriqi Arabic
notes: Proto-Mashriqi (Eastern) Arabic — abstract ancestor node for Egyptian, Sudanese, Levantine, Iraqi, and Gulf Arabic. Key shared Eastern features: short vowels generally retained; ج /dʒ/ preserved; ث → /t/, ذ → /d/ in sedentary varieties; ض → /dˤ/ (lateral lost); long vowels maintained. ORTHOGRAPHIC READING BASE: graphemes/positional_graphemes inherit from the modern MSA leaf `ar` (not Classical `arb`), because the modern spoken varieties are written in MSA orthography — so they must read it the way `ar` does: the onset-glide digraphs يَ/وَ (/ja/,/wa/, not diphthongs), the hamza carriers أ/إ as bare /ʔ/ before an explicit harakah, word-final ي/و preferring the long-vowel reading, and pausal tāʾ marbūṭa. Genealogy is unchanged (parent/ancestors still point at Classical); only the data-inheritance base moves. Each variety's own phonological overrides (interdentals, qāf reflex, affrication) sit in its own tables and continue to win over the inherited layer.

sources.author: Watson, J.C.E.
sources.title: The Phonology and Morphology of Arabic

sources.author: Holes, C.
sources.title: Modern Arabic: Structures, Functions, and Varieties
